articles.headline: BJP Secures Third Consecutive Term in Haryana Assembly Elections, Forms Government
articles.explainer:
• The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has emerged as the single largest party in the 2024 Haryana Legislative Assembly elections, securing 48 out of 90 seats, crossing the majority mark.
• This victory marks the BJP's third consecutive term in power in the state, signaling strong voter confidence in the party's governance and development agenda.
• Key issues influencing the election included agricultural policies, employment generation, and caste-based reservations, alongside national political narratives.
• The results are crucial for the BJP's regional stronghold and set a precedent for upcoming state elections, reflecting shifts in political dynamics.
• Political analysts are evaluating the performance of opposition parties and independent candidates, noting the implications for future electoral strategies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: BJP won 48 seats out of 90 in the 2024 Haryana Assembly Elections.
This is BJP's third consecutive term in Haryana.
The total strength of the Haryana Legislative Assembly is 90 seats.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to BJP's continued success in Haryana, including welfare schemes, national leadership appeal, and organizational strength.
Discuss the implications of state election results on national politics and the federal structure of India.
Examine the role of regional issues vs. national issues in determining electoral outcomes in state assembly elections.
Evaluate the performance of opposition parties and the challenges they face in countering established political narratives.
articles.imageDescription: Map of Haryana with election results showing constituencies won by different parties.
articles.trivia: Haryana was carved out of the former state of East Punjab on 1 November 1966, on the basis of language.

articles.headline: Over 36 Maoists Killed in Major Anti-Naxal Operation in Chhattisgarh's Abujhmarh Forest
articles.explainer:
• A significant anti-Maoist operation in the Abujhmarh forest area of Chhattisgarh resulted in the killing of more than 36 Maoist insurgents.
• The operation was jointly carried out by the District Reserve Guards (DRG) and the Special Task Force (STF), demonstrating enhanced inter-agency coordination.
• Abujhmarh, known as a 'liberated zone' for Maoists due to its dense forests and difficult terrain, has been a major challenge for security forces.
• This operation represents a major blow to the Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) movement in the region, impacting their leadership and logistical capabilities.
• The government continues to pursue a dual strategy of intense security operations coupled with development initiatives to counter Naxalism.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention. Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: The operation took place in the Abujhmarh forest area of Chhattisgarh.
Security forces involved were District Reserve Guards (DRG) and Special Task Force (STF).
Abujhmarh is a remote, densely forested area, historically a stronghold for Maoists.
mainsPoints: Discuss the effectiveness of security-centric approaches in tackling Left-Wing Extremism in India.
Examine the socio-economic factors that contribute to the spread of Naxalism and evaluate the government's development strategy in affected areas.
Analyze the challenges faced by security forces in conducting operations in difficult terrains like Abujhmarh and the strategies employed to overcome them.
Critically assess the long-term implications of such major operations on the overall Naxalite movement and local populations.
articles.imageDescription: Map of Chhattisgarh highlighting the Abujhmarh region, with an inset of security personnel.
articles.trivia: Abujhmarh literally means 'the unknown hills' in the local Gondi language, reflecting its historical inaccessibility.

articles.headline: Tragic Road Accident in Bhadohi, Uttar Pradesh, Claims 10 Lives, Injures Three
articles.explainer:
• A devastating collision between a truck and a tractor-trolley in Bhadohi district, Uttar Pradesh, resulted in the death of 10 individuals.
• Three others sustained serious injuries and were immediately rushed to nearby hospitals for medical attention.
• Preliminary investigations point towards potential issues of overloading, reckless driving, or poor road conditions as contributing factors.
• The incident has reignited concerns over road safety standards and the urgent need for stricter enforcement of traffic regulations in India.
• Authorities have announced ex-gratia compensation for the victims' families and initiated a probe into the exact cause of the accident.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment
Disaster and disaster management
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges to road safety in India, including infrastructure, enforcement, and public awareness.
Suggest comprehensive measures that can be adopted by the government and civil society to reduce road accidents and fatalities.
Analyze the socio-economic impact of road accidents on families and the national economy.
Critically evaluate existing policies and legal frameworks for road safety in India and propose areas for reform.
articles.imageDescription: Emergency services attending to a road accident scene in a rural setting.
articles.trivia: India accounts for about 11% of the total road accident deaths in the world, despite having only 1% of the world's vehicles.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Reiterates Focus on Inflation Control Amidst Global Volatility
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its latest meeting, deciding to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This decision aligns with the RBI's cautious stance to manage persistent inflationary pressures while supporting economic growth.
• The MPC highlighted global uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility, as key risks to the domestic inflation outlook.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das emphasized the need for continued vigilance to anchor inflation expectations firmly within the target band.
• The RBI's forward guidance indicates a data-dependent approach, with future policy actions contingent on evolving macroeconomic conditions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System—objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: The current repo rate has been maintained at 6.5%.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for setting the benchmark interest rate in India.
The primary objective of the RBI's monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Analyze the impact of an unchanged repo rate on various sectors of the Indian economy, including banking, real estate, and manufacturing.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Monetary Policy Committee's framework in achieving its objectives and suggest potential improvements.
Examine the interplay between monetary policy and fiscal policy in India and how they can be harmonized for better economic outcomes.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with its logo, symbolizing economic policy.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and consists of six members – three officials of the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India's Service Sector Activity Expands Robustly in September, PMI Data Indicates Strong Momentum
articles.explainer:
• India's services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) for September showed robust expansion, maintaining strong momentum from previous months.
• The growth was driven by a surge in new orders and improved business confidence, signaling healthy domestic demand.
• Employment in the service sector also registered an increase, pointing towards job creation amidst economic expansion.
• Despite inflationary pressures, businesses remain optimistic about future activity, buoyed by festive season demand and government infrastructure spending.
• This strong performance of the service sector complements the manufacturing growth, contributing significantly to India's overall economic resilience.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: The Services PMI is a key economic indicator that measures the health of the service sector.
A PMI reading above 50 indicates expansion, while below 50 indicates contraction.
The service sector is a major contributor to India's GDP.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key drivers of growth in India's service sector and its contribution to overall economic development.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for sustaining high growth in the service sector, including skilling, digitization, and global competition.
Examine the linkages between the service sector's performance and employment generation, particularly for different demographic groups.
Evaluate the role of government policies and reforms in fostering a conducive environment for service sector expansion.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing an upward trending line representing India's Service PMI.
articles.trivia: India's services sector accounts for over 50% of the country's GDP.

articles.headline: ISRO Releases New Insights from Chandrayaan-3 Data, Expanding Lunar Science Knowledge
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has published initial findings and data analysis from its Chandrayaan-3 mission, particularly from the Pragyan rover and Vikram lander payloads.
• New data provides crucial information on the lunar surface's thermal properties, seismic activity, and elemental composition near the South Pole.
• Scientists are analyzing the Spectro-polarimetry of Habitable Planet Earth (SHAPE) payload's observations for exoplanet research.
• This data significantly contributes to global lunar science, aiding future missions and understanding the Moon's origin and evolution.
• The success of Chandrayaan-3 continues to bolster India's position as a leading space-faring nation.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
articles.source: ISRO Website / The Times of India
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed near the lunar South Pole.
Key components included the Vikram lander and Pragyan rover.
SHAPE payload's primary objective is to study Earth's spectro-polarimetric signatures from lunar orbit.
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific objectives and key findings of India's Chandrayaan-3 mission and its contribution to lunar science.
Analyze the strategic importance of India's lunar exploration program for its space capabilities and international collaborations.
Examine the technological innovations and indigenous capabilities demonstrated by ISRO in the Chandrayaan-3 mission.
Evaluate the future prospects of India's space program, including Gaganyaan and future planetary missions, based on the success of Chandrayaan-3.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of Chandrayaan-3 lander and rover on the lunar surface.
articles.trivia: India became the fourth country to successfully land on the Moon and the first to land near the lunar South Pole with Chandrayaan-3.

articles.headline: PM-eBus Sewa Scheme Gains Momentum, Enhancing Urban Electric Mobility Across Cities
articles.explainer:
• The 'PM-eBus Sewa' scheme, aimed at augmenting city bus operations with electric buses, is showing significant progress across various Indian cities.
• The scheme facilitates the deployment of 10,000 e-buses on a public-private partnership (PPP) model, promoting green mobility and reducing carbon emissions.
• Cities are receiving support for charging infrastructure development and operational expenses, making electric public transport more viable.
• This initiative is crucial for modernizing urban transport, reducing air pollution, and achieving India's climate action goals.
• It also creates employment opportunities and fosters indigenous manufacturing of electric vehicles and components.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: The PM-eBus Sewa scheme aims to deploy 10,000 e-buses in cities.
It operates on a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model.
The scheme falls under the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of electric public transport in achieving India's sustainable development goals and combating urban air pollution.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up electric vehicle adoption in India's public transport sector, including infrastructure, cost, and technology.
Evaluate the role of government schemes like PM-eBus Sewa in driving the transition towards green mobility and fostering indigenous EV manufacturing.
Suggest policy measures to further accelerate the adoption of electric vehicles in both public and private sectors.
articles.imageDescription: A modern electric bus operating in an Indian city.
articles.trivia: India aims to achieve 30% electric vehicle penetration by 2030, a target that schemes like PM-eBus Sewa contribute to significantly.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Environmental Clearance for Key Infrastructure Project, Emphasizes Balanced Development
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India has upheld the environmental clearance granted to a major infrastructure project, dismissing petitions challenging its validity.
• The court's ruling emphasized the need for a balanced approach between environmental protection and developmental imperatives for national progress.
• The verdict highlighted that due process was followed in the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) and public consultation phases.
• Environmental activists have expressed concerns regarding the long-term ecological impact, while proponents cite economic benefits and job creation.
• This judgment reinforces the legal framework for environmental governance while allowing crucial development projects to proceed.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States; issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court plays a crucial role in interpreting environmental laws.
Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) is a mandatory process for major projects.
The concept of 'balanced development' often arises in such judicial decisions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the judiciary in balancing environmental protection and economic development in India, citing relevant case laws.
Critically analyze the effectiveness of the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process in India and suggest reforms to strengthen it.
Examine the challenges in implementing environmental regulations for large-scale infrastructure projects and the role of public participation.
Evaluate the concept of 'sustainable development' in the context of India's growth trajectory and the legal framework supporting it.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building, symbolizing justice and legal decisions.
articles.trivia: The 'polluter pays principle' and 'precautionary principle' are key tenets of environmental jurisprudence in India, often invoked in such cases.

articles.headline: India and France Conclude Strategic Dialogue, Deepening Cooperation in Defence, Space, and Green Energy
articles.explainer:
• India and France held their annual Strategic Dialogue, reaffirming their commitment to a robust bilateral partnership.
• Discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in defence manufacturing, joint military exercises, and technology transfer.
• Both nations explored new avenues for collaboration in space exploration, including satellite navigation and shared payloads.
• Green energy transition and climate change mitigation were also high on the agenda, with pledges for joint research and development.
• The dialogue underscored the shared vision for a stable and prosperous Indo-Pacific region, emphasizing maritime security and multilateralism.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora- their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India and France are strategic partners with cooperation spanning defence, space, and nuclear energy.
France is a key partner for India in the Indo-Pacific region.
Joint military exercises like 'Varuna' (naval), 'Garuda' (air), and 'Shakti' (army) are conducted between India and France.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution and significance of the India-France strategic partnership in the contemporary geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the key pillars of cooperation between India and France, particularly in defence, space, and emerging technologies, and their mutual benefits.
Examine how the India-France relationship contributes to maintaining stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate the potential for further deepening bilateral ties, especially in areas like green energy transition and cultural exchange.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and France displayed together, symbolizing diplomatic relations.
articles.trivia: India and France established a strategic partnership in 1998, which has since expanded to cover various domains.

articles.headline: National Green Tribunal Directs States to Ensure Strict Compliance with Waste Management Rules
articles.explainer:
• The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has issued fresh directives to state governments and Union Territories to ensure stringent compliance with Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016.
• The NGT expressed serious concerns over the continued neglect in scientific waste processing and disposal, leading to environmental pollution.
• States have been mandated to submit action plans for achieving 100% waste segregation, collection, and treatment within a stipulated timeframe.
• The order emphasizes the 'polluter pays' principle and the need for decentralized waste management solutions.
• This intervention aims to address the growing challenge of urban waste management and promote a circular economy approach.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: The National Green Tribunal (NGT) was established in 2010 under the National Green Tribunal Act.
The Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016, replaced the Municipal Solid Wastes (Management and Handling) Rules, 2000.
The 'polluter pays' principle is a key tenet of environmental law in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in implementing effective solid waste management practices in Indian cities and the role of the National Green Tribunal in addressing them.
Analyze the key provisions of the Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016, and their potential impact on urban sanitation and environmental health.
Examine the concept of a 'circular economy' in the context of waste management and suggest policy interventions to promote it in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of judicial interventions by bodies like the NGT in driving environmental compliance and governance.
articles.imageDescription: A depiction of waste segregation bins for different types of waste, symbolizing proper waste management.
articles.trivia: India generates approximately 1.5 lakh metric tonnes of solid waste every day.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Initiatives to Boost Millets Production and Export, Promoting Nutritional Security
articles.explainer:
• The Union government has launched a series of new initiatives aimed at significantly increasing the production and export of millets.
• These measures include enhanced Minimum Support Price (MSP) for millets, promotion of organic farming practices, and creation of export hubs.
• The move aligns with India's declaration of 2023 as the 'International Year of Millets' and its commitment to nutritional security.
• Millets are recognized for their high nutritional value, climate resilience, and low water footprint, making them a sustainable food choice.
• The initiatives also focus on value addition, processing, and integrating millets into public distribution and mid-day meal schemes.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System—objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: 2023 was declared the 'International Year of Millets' by the United Nations, at India's initiative.
Millets are often referred to as 'nutri-cereals' due to their high nutritional content.
Examples of millets include Jowar, Bajra, Ragi, Kodo, Foxtail, etc.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of millets in achieving food and nutritional security in India, considering their ecological and health benefits.
Analyze the challenges faced by millet farmers in India and evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives to boost their production and market access.
Examine how promoting millets can contribute to sustainable agriculture and climate resilience in the context of changing climatic patterns.
Suggest a comprehensive strategy for integrating millets into India's food systems, including public awareness campaigns, value chain development, and policy support.
articles.imageDescription: Various types of millets displayed in bowls, showcasing their diversity and nutritional value.
articles.trivia: India is the largest producer of millets in the world, accounting for about 41% of global production.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Significant Ancient Artifacts in Odisha, Revealing New Historical Insights
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of a trove of ancient artifacts and structural remains at an excavation site in Odisha.
• The findings include pottery, ancient tools, coins, and remnants of habitations, dating back to the early historic period.
• This discovery is expected to provide new insights into the cultural practices, trade networks, and societal structures of ancient India.
• Conservation efforts are underway to preserve the site and the unearthed artifacts for future study and public display.
• The ASI continues its crucial work in exploring and protecting India's rich archaeological heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
Artifacts often provide clues about ancient civilizations' daily life, trade, and beliefs.
Carbon dating is a common method used to determine the age of organic materials found at excavation sites.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing ancient Indian history and understanding cultural evolution.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in preserving and managing India's vast and diverse cultural heritage.
Examine the role of modern technology and interdisciplinary approaches in contemporary archaeological research in India.
Evaluate the potential of archaeological tourism in India and the measures needed to promote it responsibly while ensuring conservation.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully working at an excavation site, revealing ancient structures.
articles.trivia: The ASI was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who also became its first Director-General.

articles.headline: India Showcases Success of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) at Global Forum, Eyes Broader Adoption
articles.explainer:
• India presented the achievements and scalability of its Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) stack, including Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, at a prominent international forum.
• The presentation highlighted how DPI has revolutionized financial inclusion, public service delivery, and digital governance in India.
• Several developing nations have expressed keen interest in adopting similar models to accelerate their own digital transformation journeys.
• India is actively engaging in bilateral and multilateral dialogues to share its expertise and facilitate the implementation of DPI in partner countries.
• This initiative positions India as a leader in digital innovation and a proponent of open, inclusive, and secure digital ecosystems globally.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
articles.source: NITI Aayog / Ministry of Electronics and IT
prelimsPoints: Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker are key components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
DPI aims to provide foundational digital capabilities for various public and private services.
India is promoting its DPI model globally, particularly in developing countries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in achieving inclusive growth and enhancing governance.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in replicating India's DPI model in other developing countries, considering socio-economic and technological contexts.
Examine the role of DPI in fostering financial inclusion, digital literacy, and empowering citizens in India.
Evaluate India's 'technology diplomacy' initiatives and how the sharing of DPI can strengthen its global influence and partnerships.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing interconnected digital services like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, symbolizing India's DPI.
articles.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in August 2023, making it one of the world's fastest-growing digital payment systems.

articles.headline: Defence Acquisition Council Approves Indigenous Procurement of Naval Systems, Boosts 'Make in India' in Defence
articles.explainer:
• The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by the Defence Minister, has approved several proposals for indigenous procurement of naval systems.
• These approvals are valued at over INR 40,000 crores and include advanced missile systems, surveillance equipment, and specialized vessels.
• The move is a significant boost to the 'Make in India' initiative, reducing reliance on foreign imports and strengthening domestic defence manufacturing capabilities.
• Emphasis was placed on designing and developing cutting-edge technologies within India, fostering self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat).
• This procurement drive aims to modernize the Indian Navy and enhance its operational readiness and strategic capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is the highest decision-making body for defence procurement in India.
'Make in India' in defence aims to achieve self-reliance in military hardware.
Indigenous procurement reduces import dependence and boosts domestic industry.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of indigenization in India's defence sector and its contribution to national security and economic growth.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in achieving self-reliance in defence manufacturing, including technology transfer, R&D, and private sector participation.
Evaluate the impact of government policies like 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' on modernizing the Indian armed forces and boosting defence exports.
Examine the role of Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) in streamlining procurement processes and ensuring transparency and efficiency.
articles.imageDescription: A modern Indian naval warship, symbolizing advanced indigenous defence capabilities.
articles.trivia: The DAC was formed in 2001, following the recommendations of the Group of Ministers on reforming the National Security System.

articles.headline: WHO Calls for Global Action on Antimicrobial Resistance, India Pledges Enhanced Efforts
articles.explainer:
• The World Health Organization (WHO) has renewed its call for urgent global action to combat Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR), warning of its severe public health threat.
• India, a major hub for pharmaceutical manufacturing and a country with high antibiotic consumption, has pledged to enhance its national action plan against AMR.
• Key focus areas include surveillance, responsible use of antibiotics in human and animal health, and investment in new drug development.
• AMR poses a significant challenge to healthcare systems worldwide, potentially making common infections untreatable and jeopardizing medical procedures.
• International cooperation and a 'One Health' approach, integrating human, animal, and environmental health, are crucial for tackling this growing crisis.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
articles.source: WHO / Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) occurs when microbes evolve to resist drugs designed to kill them.
The 'One Health' approach recognizes the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health in addressing AMR.
WHO is a key international body leading global efforts against AMR.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted challenges posed by Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) to global public health and economic development, with a specific focus on India.
Analyze the 'One Health' approach as a comprehensive strategy for combating AMR and evaluate its implementation challenges in India.
Examine the role of government policies, pharmaceutical industry, and public awareness campaigns in promoting responsible antibiotic use and preventing the spread of AMR.
Suggest innovative solutions and international collaborations required to accelerate research and development of new antimicrobial drugs.
articles.imageDescription: Microbes shown resisting antibiotic drugs, illustrating Antimicrobial Resistance.
articles.trivia: AMR is estimated to cause 700,000 deaths globally each year, a figure projected to rise to 10 million by 2050 if no action is taken.

highlights: BJP secures third consecutive term in Haryana Assembly Elections, winning 48 out of 90 seats.
Over 36 Maoists killed in a major anti-Naxal operation by DRG and STF in Chhattisgarh's Abujhmarh forest.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee maintains benchmark repo rate at 6.5%, emphasizing inflation control.
ISRO releases new data insights from Chandrayaan-3 mission, advancing lunar science knowledge.
India showcases its Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) success at a global forum, attracting interest from developing nations.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 2024 Haryana Legislative Assembly elections, consider the following statements:
1. The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) won 48 out of 90 seats, securing a clear majority.
2. This marks the third consecutive term for the BJP in the state.
3. The total strength of the Haryana Legislative Assembly is 100 seats.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the BJP secured 48 seats. Statement 2 is correct as it marks their third consecutive term. Statement 3 is incorrect; the total strength of the Haryana Legislative Assembly is 90 seats, not 100.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Abujhmarh forest area', recently in news for an anti-Maoist operation, is located in which of the following states?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Abujhmarh forest area, a known stronghold for Maoist insurgents, is located in Chhattisgarh, India. A major anti-Naxal operation was conducted there by security forces.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is/are correct?
1. The MPC is responsible for setting the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.
2. The primary objective of the MPC is to achieve price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
3. The MPC consists of five members, all of whom are RBI officials.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The MPC sets the repo rate. Statement 2 is correct: Price stability (inflation targeting) is the primary objective, with growth as a secondary consideration. Statement 3 is incorrect: The MPC consists of six members – three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the Government of India.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI):
1. Aadhaar
2. Unified Payments Interface (UPI)
3. DigiLocker
Which of the above are correctly considered as core components of India's DPI?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Aadhaar (identity layer), UPI (payments layer), and DigiLocker (data exchange layer) are all fundamental components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), enabling seamless digital services and financial inclusion.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR), consider the following statements:
1. AMR occurs when microorganisms like bacteria and viruses evolve and become resistant to the drugs used to treat infections.
2. The 'One Health' approach is crucial for combating AMR, recognizing the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health.
3. India is a signatory to the global action plan on AMR initiated by the World Health Organization (WHO).
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. AMR is a natural evolutionary process exacerbated by misuse of antibiotics. The 'One Health' approach is globally recognized as essential. India is actively engaged in global efforts, including WHO's global action plan, to combat AMR.

mainsMocks.question: The recent Haryana Assembly election results highlight the evolving dynamics of state politics in India. Analyze the key factors that influence electoral outcomes in state assembly elections and discuss their implications for India's federal structure. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States; Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Haryana election outcome. Discuss factors like local issues, welfare schemes, caste equations, national party appeal, and regional leadership. Conclude by linking these factors to their impact on federal balance and state autonomy.

mainsMocks.question: Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) continues to be a significant internal security challenge in India. In light of the recent anti-Maoist operation in Abujhmarh, critically evaluate the government's two-pronged strategy of security operations and developmental interventions to combat LWE. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Linkages between development and spread of extremism; Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security; Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by acknowledging the Abujhmarh operation and the persistence of LWE. Detail the two-pronged strategy (security and development). Critically analyze the successes and limitations of each aspect, including challenges like terrain, local support, funding, and implementation gaps. Suggest ways to enhance effectiveness.

mainsMocks.question: India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) has emerged as a model for developing nations seeking digital transformation. Discuss the core components and benefits of India's DPI and analyze the challenges in replicating this model globally. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology; Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights. GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Define DPI and list core components (Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker). Explain benefits like financial inclusion, ease of governance, and innovation. Discuss challenges for global replication: data privacy concerns, regulatory frameworks, digital literacy, infrastructure disparities, and geopolitical considerations. Conclude with India's role in promoting global DPI adoption.

quickBytes.text: The Haryana Legislative Assembly has 90 seats, and a simple majority requires 46 seats.
Abujhmarh, a region in Chhattisgarh, is characterized by dense forests and has historically been a stronghold for Maoist insurgents.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of RBI has six members: three from RBI and three external members.
The 'International Year of Millets' was declared for 2023 by the United Nations, at India's proposal.
Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed near the lunar South Pole, making India the first country to achieve this.
The National Green Tribunal (NGT) was established in 2010 for effective and expeditious disposal of environmental cases.
The 'One Health' approach for Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) integrates human, animal, and environmental health.
The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is the highest decision-making body of the Ministry of Defence for capital acquisitions.
The PM-eBus Sewa scheme aims to deploy 10,000 electric buses in 100 cities on a PPP model.
Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is responsible for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.

GS1.headline: Tragic Road Accident in Bhadohi, Uttar Pradesh, Claims 10 Lives, Injures Three
GS1.explainer:
• A devastating collision between a truck and a tractor-trolley in Bhadohi district, Uttar Pradesh, resulted in the death of 10 individuals.
• Three others sustained serious injuries and were immediately rushed to nearby hospitals for medical attention.
• Preliminary investigations point towards potential issues of overloading, reckless driving, or poor road conditions as contributing factors.
• The incident has reignited concerns over road safety standards and the urgent need for stricter enforcement of traffic regulations in India.
• Authorities have announced ex-gratia compensation for the victims' families and initiated a probe into the exact cause of the accident.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment
Disaster and disaster management
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges to road safety in India, including infrastructure, enforcement, and public awareness.
Suggest comprehensive measures that can be adopted by the government and civil society to reduce road accidents and fatalities.
Analyze the socio-economic impact of road accidents on families and the national economy.
Critically evaluate existing policies and legal frameworks for road safety in India and propose areas for reform.
GS1.imageDescription: Emergency services attending to a road accident scene in a rural setting.
GS1.trivia: India accounts for about 11% of the total road accident deaths in the world, despite having only 1% of the world's vehicles.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Significant Ancient Artifacts in Odisha, Revealing New Historical Insights
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of a trove of ancient artifacts and structural remains at an excavation site in Odisha.
• The findings include pottery, ancient tools, coins, and remnants of habitations, dating back to the early historic period.
• This discovery is expected to provide new insights into the cultural practices, trade networks, and societal structures of ancient India.
• Conservation efforts are underway to preserve the site and the unearthed artifacts for future study and public display.
• The ASI continues its crucial work in exploring and protecting India's rich archaeological heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.
Artifacts often provide clues about ancient civilizations' daily life, trade, and beliefs.
Carbon dating is a common method used to determine the age of organic materials found at excavation sites.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing ancient Indian history and understanding cultural evolution.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in preserving and managing India's vast and diverse cultural heritage.
Examine the role of modern technology and interdisciplinary approaches in contemporary archaeological research in India.
Evaluate the potential of archaeological tourism in India and the measures needed to promote it responsibly while ensuring conservation.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully working at an excavation site, revealing ancient structures.
GS1.trivia: The ASI was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who also became its first Director-General.

GS2.headline: BJP Secures Third Consecutive Term in Haryana Assembly Elections, Forms Government
GS2.explainer:
• The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has emerged as the single largest party in the 2024 Haryana Legislative Assembly elections, securing 48 out of 90 seats, crossing the majority mark.
• This victory marks the BJP's third consecutive term in power in the state, signaling strong voter confidence in the party's governance and development agenda.
• Key issues influencing the election included agricultural policies, employment generation, and caste-based reservations, alongside national political narratives.
• The results are crucial for the BJP's regional stronghold and set a precedent for upcoming state elections, reflecting shifts in political dynamics.
• Political analysts are evaluating the performance of opposition parties and independent candidates, noting the implications for future electoral strategies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: BJP won 48 seats out of 90 in the 2024 Haryana Assembly Elections.
This is BJP's third consecutive term in Haryana.
The total strength of the Haryana Legislative Assembly is 90 seats.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to BJP's continued success in Haryana, including welfare schemes, national leadership appeal, and organizational strength.
Discuss the implications of state election results on national politics and the federal structure of India.
Examine the role of regional issues vs. national issues in determining electoral outcomes in state assembly elections.
Evaluate the performance of opposition parties and the challenges they face in countering established political narratives.
GS2.imageDescription: Map of Haryana with election results showing constituencies won by different parties.
GS2.trivia: Haryana was carved out of the former state of East Punjab on 1 November 1966, on the basis of language.

GS2.headline: PM-eBus Sewa Scheme Gains Momentum, Enhancing Urban Electric Mobility Across Cities
GS2.explainer:
• The 'PM-eBus Sewa' scheme, aimed at augmenting city bus operations with electric buses, is showing significant progress across various Indian cities.
• The scheme facilitates the deployment of 10,000 e-buses on a public-private partnership (PPP) model, promoting green mobility and reducing carbon emissions.
• Cities are receiving support for charging infrastructure development and operational expenses, making electric public transport more viable.
• This initiative is crucial for modernizing urban transport, reducing air pollution, and achieving India's climate action goals.
• It also creates employment opportunities and fosters indigenous manufacturing of electric vehicles and components.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
GS2.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: The PM-eBus Sewa scheme aims to deploy 10,000 e-buses in cities.
It operates on a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model.
The scheme falls under the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of electric public transport in achieving India's sustainable development goals and combating urban air pollution.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up electric vehicle adoption in India's public transport sector, including infrastructure, cost, and technology.
Evaluate the role of government schemes like PM-eBus Sewa in driving the transition towards green mobility and fostering indigenous EV manufacturing.
Suggest policy measures to further accelerate the adoption of electric vehicles in both public and private sectors.
GS2.imageDescription: A modern electric bus operating in an Indian city.
GS2.trivia: India aims to achieve 30% electric vehicle penetration by 2030, a target that schemes like PM-eBus Sewa contribute to significantly.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Environmental Clearance for Key Infrastructure Project, Emphasizes Balanced Development
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India has upheld the environmental clearance granted to a major infrastructure project, dismissing petitions challenging its validity.
• The court's ruling emphasized the need for a balanced approach between environmental protection and developmental imperatives for national progress.
• The verdict highlighted that due process was followed in the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) and public consultation phases.
• Environmental activists have expressed concerns regarding the long-term ecological impact, while proponents cite economic benefits and job creation.
• This judgment reinforces the legal framework for environmental governance while allowing crucial development projects to proceed.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States; issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court plays a crucial role in interpreting environmental laws.
Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) is a mandatory process for major projects.
The concept of 'balanced development' often arises in such judicial decisions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the judiciary in balancing environmental protection and economic development in India, citing relevant case laws.
Critically analyze the effectiveness of the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process in India and suggest reforms to strengthen it.
Examine the challenges in implementing environmental regulations for large-scale infrastructure projects and the role of public participation.
Evaluate the concept of 'sustainable development' in the context of India's growth trajectory and the legal framework supporting it.
GS2.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building, symbolizing justice and legal decisions.
GS2.trivia: The 'polluter pays principle' and 'precautionary principle' are key tenets of environmental jurisprudence in India, often invoked in such cases.

GS2.headline: India and France Conclude Strategic Dialogue, Deepening Cooperation in Defence, Space, and Green Energy
GS2.explainer:
• India and France held their annual Strategic Dialogue, reaffirming their commitment to a robust bilateral partnership.
• Discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in defence manufacturing, joint military exercises, and technology transfer.
• Both nations explored new avenues for collaboration in space exploration, including satellite navigation and shared payloads.
• Green energy transition and climate change mitigation were also high on the agenda, with pledges for joint research and development.
• The dialogue underscored the shared vision for a stable and prosperous Indo-Pacific region, emphasizing maritime security and multilateralism.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora- their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India and France are strategic partners with cooperation spanning defence, space, and nuclear energy.
France is a key partner for India in the Indo-Pacific region.
Joint military exercises like 'Varuna' (naval), 'Garuda' (air), and 'Shakti' (army) are conducted between India and France.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution and significance of the India-France strategic partnership in the contemporary geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the key pillars of cooperation between India and France, particularly in defence, space, and emerging technologies, and their mutual benefits.
Examine how the India-France relationship contributes to maintaining stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate the potential for further deepening bilateral ties, especially in areas like green energy transition and cultural exchange.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and France displayed together, symbolizing diplomatic relations.
GS2.trivia: India and France established a strategic partnership in 1998, which has since expanded to cover various domains.

GS3.headline: Over 36 Maoists Killed in Major Anti-Naxal Operation in Chhattisgarh's Abujhmarh Forest
GS3.explainer:
• A significant anti-Maoist operation in the Abujhmarh forest area of Chhattisgarh resulted in the killing of more than 36 Maoist insurgents.
• The operation was jointly carried out by the District Reserve Guards (DRG) and the Special Task Force (STF), demonstrating enhanced inter-agency coordination.
• Abujhmarh, known as a 'liberated zone' for Maoists due to its dense forests and difficult terrain, has been a major challenge for security forces.
• This operation represents a major blow to the Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) movement in the region, impacting their leadership and logistical capabilities.
• The government continues to pursue a dual strategy of intense security operations coupled with development initiatives to counter Naxalism.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention. Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: The operation took place in the Abujhmarh forest area of Chhattisgarh.
Security forces involved were District Reserve Guards (DRG) and Special Task Force (STF).
Abujhmarh is a remote, densely forested area, historically a stronghold for Maoists.
mainsPoints: Discuss the effectiveness of security-centric approaches in tackling Left-Wing Extremism in India.
Examine the socio-economic factors that contribute to the spread of Naxalism and evaluate the government's development strategy in affected areas.
Analyze the challenges faced by security forces in conducting operations in difficult terrains like Abujhmarh and the strategies employed to overcome them.
Critically assess the long-term implications of such major operations on the overall Naxalite movement and local populations.
GS3.imageDescription: Map of Chhattisgarh highlighting the Abujhmarh region, with an inset of security personnel.
GS3.trivia: Abujhmarh literally means 'the unknown hills' in the local Gondi language, reflecting its historical inaccessibility.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Reiterates Focus on Inflation Control Amidst Global Volatility
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its latest meeting, deciding to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This decision aligns with the RBI's cautious stance to manage persistent inflationary pressures while supporting economic growth.
• The MPC highlighted global uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions and commodity price volatility, as key risks to the domestic inflation outlook.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das emphasized the need for continued vigilance to anchor inflation expectations firmly within the target band.
• The RBI's forward guidance indicates a data-dependent approach, with future policy actions contingent on evolving macroeconomic conditions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System—objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: The current repo rate has been maintained at 6.5%.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for setting the benchmark interest rate in India.
The primary objective of the RBI's monetary policy is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Analyze the impact of an unchanged repo rate on various sectors of the Indian economy, including banking, real estate, and manufacturing.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Monetary Policy Committee's framework in achieving its objectives and suggest potential improvements.
Examine the interplay between monetary policy and fiscal policy in India and how they can be harmonized for better economic outcomes.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with its logo, symbolizing economic policy.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and consists of six members – three officials of the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: India's Service Sector Activity Expands Robustly in September, PMI Data Indicates Strong Momentum
GS3.explainer:
• India's services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) for September showed robust expansion, maintaining strong momentum from previous months.
• The growth was driven by a surge in new orders and improved business confidence, signaling healthy domestic demand.
• Employment in the service sector also registered an increase, pointing towards job creation amidst economic expansion.
• Despite inflationary pressures, businesses remain optimistic about future activity, buoyed by festive season demand and government infrastructure spending.
• This strong performance of the service sector complements the manufacturing growth, contributing significantly to India's overall economic resilience.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: The Services PMI is a key economic indicator that measures the health of the service sector.
A PMI reading above 50 indicates expansion, while below 50 indicates contraction.
The service sector is a major contributor to India's GDP.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key drivers of growth in India's service sector and its contribution to overall economic development.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for sustaining high growth in the service sector, including skilling, digitization, and global competition.
Examine the linkages between the service sector's performance and employment generation, particularly for different demographic groups.
Evaluate the role of government policies and reforms in fostering a conducive environment for service sector expansion.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic showing an upward trending line representing India's Service PMI.
GS3.trivia: India's services sector accounts for over 50% of the country's GDP.

GS3.headline: ISRO Releases New Insights from Chandrayaan-3 Data, Expanding Lunar Science Knowledge
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has published initial findings and data analysis from its Chandrayaan-3 mission, particularly from the Pragyan rover and Vikram lander payloads.
• New data provides crucial information on the lunar surface's thermal properties, seismic activity, and elemental composition near the South Pole.
• Scientists are analyzing the Spectro-polarimetry of Habitable Planet Earth (SHAPE) payload's observations for exoplanet research.
• This data significantly contributes to global lunar science, aiding future missions and understanding the Moon's origin and evolution.
• The success of Chandrayaan-3 continues to bolster India's position as a leading space-faring nation.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
GS3.source: ISRO Website / The Times of India
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed near the lunar South Pole.
Key components included the Vikram lander and Pragyan rover.
SHAPE payload's primary objective is to study Earth's spectro-polarimetric signatures from lunar orbit.
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific objectives and key findings of India's Chandrayaan-3 mission and its contribution to lunar science.
Analyze the strategic importance of India's lunar exploration program for its space capabilities and international collaborations.
Examine the technological innovations and indigenous capabilities demonstrated by ISRO in the Chandrayaan-3 mission.
Evaluate the future prospects of India's space program, including Gaganyaan and future planetary missions, based on the success of Chandrayaan-3.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of Chandrayaan-3 lander and rover on the lunar surface.
GS3.trivia: India became the fourth country to successfully land on the Moon and the first to land near the lunar South Pole with Chandrayaan-3.

GS3.headline: National Green Tribunal Directs States to Ensure Strict Compliance with Waste Management Rules
GS3.explainer:
• The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has issued fresh directives to state governments and Union Territories to ensure stringent compliance with Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016.
• The NGT expressed serious concerns over the continued neglect in scientific waste processing and disposal, leading to environmental pollution.
• States have been mandated to submit action plans for achieving 100% waste segregation, collection, and treatment within a stipulated timeframe.
• The order emphasizes the 'polluter pays' principle and the need for decentralized waste management solutions.
• This intervention aims to address the growing challenge of urban waste management and promote a circular economy approach.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: The National Green Tribunal (NGT) was established in 2010 under the National Green Tribunal Act.
The Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016, replaced the Municipal Solid Wastes (Management and Handling) Rules, 2000.
The 'polluter pays' principle is a key tenet of environmental law in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in implementing effective solid waste management practices in Indian cities and the role of the National Green Tribunal in addressing them.
Analyze the key provisions of the Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016, and their potential impact on urban sanitation and environmental health.
Examine the concept of a 'circular economy' in the context of waste management and suggest policy interventions to promote it in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of judicial interventions by bodies like the NGT in driving environmental compliance and governance.
GS3.imageDescription: A depiction of waste segregation bins for different types of waste, symbolizing proper waste management.
GS3.trivia: India generates approximately 1.5 lakh metric tonnes of solid waste every day.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Initiatives to Boost Millets Production and Export, Promoting Nutritional Security
GS3.explainer:
• The Union government has launched a series of new initiatives aimed at significantly increasing the production and export of millets.
• These measures include enhanced Minimum Support Price (MSP) for millets, promotion of organic farming practices, and creation of export hubs.
• The move aligns with India's declaration of 2023 as the 'International Year of Millets' and its commitment to nutritional security.
• Millets are recognized for their high nutritional value, climate resilience, and low water footprint, making them a sustainable food choice.
• The initiatives also focus on value addition, processing, and integrating millets into public distribution and mid-day meal schemes.
gsSubTopics: Major crops - cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System—objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: 2023 was declared the 'International Year of Millets' by the United Nations, at India's initiative.
Millets are often referred to as 'nutri-cereals' due to their high nutritional content.
Examples of millets include Jowar, Bajra, Ragi, Kodo, Foxtail, etc.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of millets in achieving food and nutritional security in India, considering their ecological and health benefits.
Analyze the challenges faced by millet farmers in India and evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives to boost their production and market access.
Examine how promoting millets can contribute to sustainable agriculture and climate resilience in the context of changing climatic patterns.
Suggest a comprehensive strategy for integrating millets into India's food systems, including public awareness campaigns, value chain development, and policy support.
GS3.imageDescription: Various types of millets displayed in bowls, showcasing their diversity and nutritional value.
GS3.trivia: India is the largest producer of millets in the world, accounting for about 41% of global production.

GS3.headline: India Showcases Success of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) at Global Forum, Eyes Broader Adoption
GS3.explainer:
• India presented the achievements and scalability of its Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) stack, including Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, at a prominent international forum.
• The presentation highlighted how DPI has revolutionized financial inclusion, public service delivery, and digital governance in India.
• Several developing nations have expressed keen interest in adopting similar models to accelerate their own digital transformation journeys.
• India is actively engaging in bilateral and multilateral dialogues to share its expertise and facilitate the implementation of DPI in partner countries.
• This initiative positions India as a leader in digital innovation and a proponent of open, inclusive, and secure digital ecosystems globally.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
GS3.source: NITI Aayog / Ministry of Electronics and IT
prelimsPoints: Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker are key components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
DPI aims to provide foundational digital capabilities for various public and private services.
India is promoting its DPI model globally, particularly in developing countries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in achieving inclusive growth and enhancing governance.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in replicating India's DPI model in other developing countries, considering socio-economic and technological contexts.
Examine the role of DPI in fostering financial inclusion, digital literacy, and empowering citizens in India.
Evaluate India's 'technology diplomacy' initiatives and how the sharing of DPI can strengthen its global influence and partnerships.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic showing interconnected digital services like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, symbolizing India's DPI.
GS3.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in August 2023, making it one of the world's fastest-growing digital payment systems.

GS3.headline: Defence Acquisition Council Approves Indigenous Procurement of Naval Systems, Boosts 'Make in India' in Defence
GS3.explainer:
• The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by the Defence Minister, has approved several proposals for indigenous procurement of naval systems.
• These approvals are valued at over INR 40,000 crores and include advanced missile systems, surveillance equipment, and specialized vessels.
• The move is a significant boost to the 'Make in India' initiative, reducing reliance on foreign imports and strengthening domestic defence manufacturing capabilities.
• Emphasis was placed on designing and developing cutting-edge technologies within India, fostering self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat).
• This procurement drive aims to modernize the Indian Navy and enhance its operational readiness and strategic capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) is the highest decision-making body for defence procurement in India.
'Make in India' in defence aims to achieve self-reliance in military hardware.
Indigenous procurement reduces import dependence and boosts domestic industry.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of indigenization in India's defence sector and its contribution to national security and economic growth.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in achieving self-reliance in defence manufacturing, including technology transfer, R&D, and private sector participation.
Evaluate the impact of government policies like 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' on modernizing the Indian armed forces and boosting defence exports.
Examine the role of Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) in streamlining procurement processes and ensuring transparency and efficiency.
GS3.imageDescription: A modern Indian naval warship, symbolizing advanced indigenous defence capabilities.
GS3.trivia: The DAC was formed in 2001, following the recommendations of the Group of Ministers on reforming the National Security System.

GS3.headline: WHO Calls for Global Action on Antimicrobial Resistance, India Pledges Enhanced Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• The World Health Organization (WHO) has renewed its call for urgent global action to combat Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR), warning of its severe public health threat.
• India, a major hub for pharmaceutical manufacturing and a country with high antibiotic consumption, has pledged to enhance its national action plan against AMR.
• Key focus areas include surveillance, responsible use of antibiotics in human and animal health, and investment in new drug development.
• AMR poses a significant challenge to healthcare systems worldwide, potentially making common infections untreatable and jeopardizing medical procedures.
• International cooperation and a 'One Health' approach, integrating human, animal, and environmental health, are crucial for tackling this growing crisis.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
GS3.source: WHO / Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) occurs when microbes evolve to resist drugs designed to kill them.
The 'One Health' approach recognizes the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health in addressing AMR.
WHO is a key international body leading global efforts against AMR.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted challenges posed by Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) to global public health and economic development, with a specific focus on India.
Analyze the 'One Health' approach as a comprehensive strategy for combating AMR and evaluate its implementation challenges in India.
Examine the role of government policies, pharmaceutical industry, and public awareness campaigns in promoting responsible antibiotic use and preventing the spread of AMR.
Suggest innovative solutions and international collaborations required to accelerate research and development of new antimicrobial drugs.
GS3.imageDescription: Microbes shown resisting antibiotic drugs, illustrating Antimicrobial Resistance.
GS3.trivia: AMR is estimated to cause 700,000 deaths globally each year, a figure projected to rise to 10 million by 2050 if no action is taken.

sources: ISRO Website / The Times of India
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
NITI Aayog / Ministry of Electronics and IT
WHO / Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
